event: Angel for the Chapelle St. Pierre at Villefranche-sur-Mer
symbols: angel with flowing lines — the messenger of God rendered in modern, fluid form
blue wash background — the sea of Villefranche and the heavenly realm
text referencing Chapelle St. Pierre — the chapel dedicated to the fisherman-apostle
description: A minimalist line drawing on a blue watercolor wash background. A stylized angel figure is rendered in bold, curving black outlines, with sweeping wings and a covered head. The figure holds small objects (possibly fish or keys) in its hands. Handwritten text in French reads 'Villefranche sur mer Chapelle St Pierre' along with the artist's signature 'Jean Cocteau' at the bottom. The style is modern and fluid, with few details but strong gestural lines.
theological_description: This design by Jean Cocteau relates to his decoration of the Chapelle Saint-Pierre in Villefranche-sur-Mer, a fishermen's chapel dedicated to Saint Peter. The angel depicted connects to the rich biblical tradition of angelic messengers. Peter, the 'rock' upon which Christ built his Church (Matthew 16:18), was himself a fisherman called from his nets by Jesus (Matthew 4:18-19). An angel freed Peter from prison in Acts 12:7-10, a miraculous intervention that resonates with the protective angelic presence shown here. The modernist rendering, stripped to essential flowing lines, reflects a contemporary theology of sacred art — that the divine can be communicated through simplicity and gesture rather than ornate realism. The angel's presence over a fishermen's chapel recalls Jesus's promise to Peter and Andrew: 'Come, follow me, and I will send you out to fish for people' (Matthew 4:19).

scripture_refs.reason: The chapel is dedicated to Saint Peter, the rock on whom Christ built his Church
An angel freed Peter from prison, connecting angelic protection with the apostle Peter
Peter the fisherman called by Christ — the chapel's dedication to a fishermen's patron saint
